name: Miniature Tex-Mex Egg Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. FILLING: In a large skillet, cook ground beef, onion, and green pepper until the meat is brown and vegetables are tender. Drain off excess fat.
2. Stir in beans, shredded cheese, catsup, chili powder, and cumin into the meat mixture.
3. Place a wonton skin with one point toward you. Spoon a generous teaspoon of filling across the center of the skin.
4. Fold the bottom point of the skin over the filling. Tuck the point under the filling.
5. Fold the side corners over to form a triangle shape. Moisten the pointed end; press to seal.
6. Repeat with remaining fillings and skins.
7. Fry egg rolls, a few at a time, in deep hot oil (375°F) about 1 minute on each side or until golden brown.
8. Use a slotted spoon to remove egg rolls and drain on paper towels.
9. Serve warm with taco sauce. Makes 48.
10. May be frozen and placed in an oven preheated to 350°F, loosely covered for 10 to 12 minutes or until heated through.
